panels.description: Wide shot: Alice, a young girl in a blue dress, stands next to a small, ornate three-legged glass table. A single tiny golden key rests on the table. The setting is a dimly lit, large hall with several imposing doors in the background. Focus is on the table and key. Atmosphere is one of searching and minor frustration.

bubbles.text: Suddenly she came upon a little three-legged table, all made of solid glass; there was nothing on it except a tiny golden key...

panels.description: Medium shot, slightly low angle: Alice kneels before a very small, previously unnoticed, low curtain covering a tiny door (about 15 inches high). She is inserting the golden key into the lock with a look of hopeful excitement. The background suggests the continuation of the dark hall.

bubbles.text: However, on the second time round, she came upon a low curtain she had not noticed before, and behind it was a little door... to her great delight it fitted!
She opened the door and found that it led into a small passage, not much larger than a rat-hole: she looked along into the loveliest garden you ever saw.

panels.description: Split panel/focus shift: Left half shows Alice peering through the small doorway, longing expression, seeing a vibrant, sunlit 'Garden' full of bright flowers and fountains (implied beauty). Right half shows her stuck, body emphasized as too large for the opening. Soft light contrast between the dark hall and the garden view.

bubbles.text: How she longed to get out of that dark hall... but she could not even get her head through the doorway.

panels.description: Medium shot: Alice stands back in the main hall, looking towards the area where the table is. She has a thoughtful, slightly determined expression, having resigned herself to finding another solution. Shadows are soft but present, indicating contemplation.

bubbles.text: For, you see, so many out-of-the-way things had happened lately, that Alice had begun to think that very few things indeed were really impossible.
There seemed to be no use in waiting by the little door, so she went back to the table...

panels.description: Close-up on the glass table. A small, corked bottle with a crisp paper label reading 'DRINK ME' in bold letters is prominently displayed. Alice's hand hovers tentatively over it, reflecting curiosity mixed with caution. Lighting highlights the label.

bubbles.text: This time she found a little bottle on it... with the words 'DRINK ME,' beautifully printed on it in large letters.
It was all very well to say 'Drink me,' but the wise little Alice was not going to do that in a hurry.

panels.description: Medium close-up on Alice's face. Her expression is serious and reflective, perhaps looking slightly off-panel towards a memory. Subtle visual effect suggesting cautionary mental images (faint shadow of fire or sharp object).

bubbles.text: for she had read several nice little histories about children who had got burnt, and eaten up by wild beasts...
such as, that a red-hot poker will burn you... and that if you cut your finger very deeply with a knife, it usually bleeds...

panels.description: Dynamic medium shot showing Alice rapidly shrinking while holding the now empty bottle, looking down at her feet, which are rapidly approaching the floor. Her expression shifts from tentative taste to sudden shock and then amusement. Energetic line work emphasizes the rapid change in size.

bubbles.text: however, this bottle was not marked 'poison,' so Alice ventured to taste it, and finding it very nice...
said Alice; 'I must be shutting up like a telescope.'
And so it was indeed: I'm the right size for that lovely garden now!
